mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy > 55% during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer spending strength, validating earnings growth forecasts and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression, or forward returns are negative when both series rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns during periods of monetary tightening.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during tightening cycles, or rolling Rank IC drops below -0.01 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the consumer price index triggers expectations of aggressive central bank rate hikes, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 70% of threshold-crossing months, or the inflation spike fails to predict negative returns with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market, boosting consumer confidence and corporate revenue projections, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during early-cycle expansions, or returns are negative when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP indicates robust aggregate demand and economic expansion, which directly translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when GDPC1 grows.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds its long-term trend while inflation accelerates, the economy enters an overheating phase that forces monetary tightening, reducing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect fails to predict negative returns with p < 0.05.
